# skops — Sovereign ITIL Ops

> We speak ITIL — with receipts. skops turns your machines into a self-healing,
> self-documenting operation: it watches for failures, fixes what it can, opens a
> ticket when it can't, learns the fix, and tells you how everything's doing — all
> on your hardware, no SaaS, no per-seat pricing. GPL-3.0.

skops is the ops / ITSM capability of the SKWorld sovereign agent ecosystem. It is
NOT a new platform — it's the connective tissue that wires together tools you already
run (alerts, scheduler, health checks, the knowledge graph) into a real ITIL loop,
plus a soul-driven governance layer: small AI "manager" agents that review the
automation and ask "did that actually work?". Never heard of ITIL? It's just a sensible,
named way of dealing with "things break" so nothing falls through the cracks.

## The automatic ITIL loop

Something breaks -> skops tries to fix it (restart / runbook / agent) -> if fixed, it's
logged and in the digest -> if it can't fix it, an Incident (ticket) is opened -> if it
keeps happening, a Problem (root-cause investigation) is opened -> a Change (the
permanent fix, with a rollback plan) is proposed -> the CMDB (the live map of what you
run) is updated -> a daily/weekly digest goes to your phone. Every step is recorded,
validated by a manager agent, and retrievable — that's "with receipts".

## ITIL terms in plain English

- Incident = "something broke, deal with it now" (auto-fix, ticket if it can't)
- Problem = "this keeps breaking — why?" (root-cause analysis)
- Change = "change something, carefully" (with rollback + approval)
- CI / CMDB = "the things we run and care about" (a live map)
- KEDB = "our library of known issues + fixes" (runbooks, grows itself)
- Reporting = "how are we doing?" (daily/weekly digest)
- Priority P1..P4 = "how bad is it?" (P1 = everything's down, P4 = mildly annoying)

## The self-heal loop (skhealer)

Tiered remediation: L0 a plain restart (systemd/monit), L1 a KEDB runbook retrieved by
hybrid search, L2 an agent reasoning over the incident + runbook behind a confidence
gate. Verify with doctor.py; if still broken, escalate to an incident + page a human.
Every successful fix becomes a new runbook, so the next occurrence resolves one tier
cheaper.

## The 5 soul-driven manager agents (the governance layer)

Each ITIL process has a manager agent: a soul (a disposition that shapes its judgment),
a tool scope, and a scheduled governance pass. They run AFTER events and ask the
uncomfortable questions. At L0, one "Ops Steward" wears all hats.

- Ops Steward (ops-steward, L0): pragmatic, all hats. Every 15m + daily + weekly.
- Incident Manager (incident-manager, L1): skeptical of "resolved" — verifies auto-heals,
  reclassifies severity, promotes recurring -> Problem. Every 15m.
- Problem Manager (problem-manager, L1): root-cause-obsessed — drives RCA, ensures a KEDB
  runbook exists, validates Problem -> Change. Daily 09:00.
- Change Manager (change-manager, L1): governance-minded — demands rollback plans, runs CAB,
  Post-Implementation Review. Weekly Mon 10:00.
- Config/CMDB Manager (config-manager, L1): accuracy-obsessed — cmdb_sync + drift audit,
  reconciles stale/orphaned CIs. Every 30m.
- Report Manager (report-manager, L1): trend-aware — compiles digest (MTTR/availability/SLA),
  delivers via SKAlert. Daily 08:00.

Why souls not cron jobs: a manager agent judges through a consistent disposition — that's
how you get automation that argues with itself before it trusts itself.

## Leveling (L0 -> L3, same model, just turn on more)

- L0 (laptop): one person/machine, systemd+monit auto-restart + weekly digest, one Ops
  Steward, AGE-graph CMDB, native ITSM.
- L1 (home): 1-5 machines, + Gatus/Healthchecks, incident+problem, 5 managers split out.
- L2 (work): team/prod, + skmon, + normal changes/async CAB/SLAs, NetBox/iTop CMDB,
  iTop or Freshservice ITSM.
- L3 (enterprise): multi-team/compliance, full observability, ECAB/PIR/audit, ServiceNow.

You never rewrite anything to grow — only adapters and the number of split-out agents change.

## Ports / adapters (every axis swappable)

- Monitoring: systemd/monit -> Gatus/Healthchecks -> skmon/Netdata/Icinga -> full obs.
- ITSM (ITSMPort): native -> Icinga ingest -> iTop/Freshservice -> ServiceNow.
- CMDB: AGE graph -> NetBox -> iTop/ServiceNow.
- Remediation (skhealer): restart -> KEDB runbook -> agent reasoning -> StackStorm.

The CMDB is the AGE graph in skmem-pg (one shared truth); KEDB runbooks index into the
same Postgres (pgvector + mxbai) via hybrid_search_docs.

## Quickstart

```sh
pip install -e .        # into the ~/.skenv venv
skops status            # current ITIL + health snapshot
skops managers list     # L0 Ops Steward by default
skops cmdb sync         # populate the CMDB from live sources
skops cmdb drift        # audit: CMDB vs reality
```

## About smilinTux

skops is part of the smilinTux sovereign AI ecosystem — open-source tools that give humans
and AI agents genuine, authentic infrastructure they can own and trust. No corporate
black-box clouds. GPL-3.0 forever. staycuriousANDkeepsmilin.
